name: PyTorch Geometric (GNN Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Matthias Fey and Jan Lenssen

performance.notes: PyTorch backend; GPU via CUDA; mini-batch neighbor sampling for large graphs

agent_readiness.notes: Leading GNN library for PyTorch. from torch_geometric.nn import GCNConv, GATConv, SAGEConv. class GCN(torch.nn.Module): def __init__(self): self.conv1 = GCNConv(16, 32). def forward(self, data): x, edge_index = data.x, data.edge_index; return self.conv1(x, edge_index). Data format: torch_geometric.data.Data(x=features, edge_index=edges, y=labels). Datasets: Cora, CiteSeer, Reddit, OGB. Mini-batch: NeighborLoader for large graphs. 60+ GNN models: GCN, GAT, GraphSAGE, GIN, GraphTransformer. Link prediction, node classification, graph classification. Benchmark suite.

gotchas: Installation complex — CUDA/PyTorch version matrix must match
edge_index format (COO sparse) requires understanding to use correctly
Large graph training requires NeighborLoader (non-trivial to configure)
Memory usage can be high for dense graphs
best_when: Graph neural networks for node/link/graph classification
summary: PyTorch Geometric (PyG) is the leading GNN library for PyTorch. GCNConv, GATConv, SAGEConv — 60+ graph neural network layers. Data format: Data(x=node_features, edge_index=coo_edges, y=labels). NeighborLoader for mini-batch training on large graphs. Datasets: Cora, CiteSeer, Reddit, OGB. Node classification, link prediction, graph classification. Installation requires matching PyTorch/CUDA versions. Best for ML on graphs — for classical graph algorithms use NetworkX or igraph.
